old_claim.note: Bills of Exchange & Early Finance is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: The muda was organized through state-owned galleys, auctions, routes, and sailing dates; bills of exchange were not a technical prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Galley-trade section: describes maneuverable armed galleys traveling in convoy and the state system that set destinations and sailing dates, made permanent for the muda in 1329.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Venice made its state-scheduled system of armed merchant-galley convoys a permanent institution in 1329.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. The muda was organized through state-owned galleys, auctions, routes, and sailing dates; bills of exchange were not a technical prerequisite.

ontology_before: merchant_convoy_systems treated bills_of_exchange_early_finance as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: merchant_convoy_systems is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims bills_of_exchange_early_finance as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: merchant_convoy_systems no longer directly depends on bills_of_exchange_early_finance.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current merchant_convoy_systems scope technically requires bills_of_exchange_early_finance.
short_reason: The muda was organized through state-owned galleys, auctions, routes, and sailing dates; bills of exchange were not a technical prerequisite.
